posts.text:
Criminal groups in Mexico control territory, extort money from businesses and periodically paralyse entire cities. Yet the gangs are not the biggest security risk facing the World Cup
 https://www.economist.com/the-americas/2026/06/07/the-world-cup-will-test-mexicos-control-over-its-territory?taid=6a271a95538c4300013cf9fb&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text:
Margin debt in China is skyrocketing:

China's stock market margin debt is up to a record ~$431 billion.

This figure has DOUBLED over the last 2 years.

This now surpasses the 2015 Chinese stock market bubble peak of ~$354 billion by 22%.

Furthermore, margin trading volume in the Shanghai Stock Exchange has averaged $22 billion per day over the last 2 weeks, near the highest on record.

Margin trading volume has tripled over the last 12 months and is in-line with the 2015 peak levels.

Risk appetite in China is through the roof.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter
